sanskrit: समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः । शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः ॥
hindiTranslation: जो शत्रु और मित्रमें तथा मानअपमानमें सम है और शीतउष्ण अनुकूलताप्रतिकूलता तथा सुखदुःखमें सम है एवं आसक्तिसे रहित है, और जो निन्दास्तुतिको समान समझनेवाला, मननशील, जिसकिसी प्रकारसे भी शरीरका निर्वाह होनेमें संतुष्ट, रहनेके स्थान तथा शरीरमें ममताआसक्तिसे रहित और स्थिर बुद्धिवाला है, वह भक्तिमान् मनुष्य मुझे प्रिय है ॥
englishTranslation: One who is equal to enemy and friend, honor and dishonor, heat and cold, pleasure and pain, and is free from attachment is dear to Me.
essence: Real devotion stays even when life feels hostile or kind.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A devoted person stays steady through praise and blame, comfort and discomfort, friendship and hostility, without clinging to either side.
simpleInsight: You can stay open and calm without needing life to treat you kindly.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still describing the devotee Arjuna should become. After listing inner qualities in the battlefield crisis, he adds one more test: the beloved devotee does not wobble under praise, insult, comfort, or pain.
modernRelevance: A message lands as criticism, then another person praises you five minutes later. Your mood swings with both. This verse trains you to stay unchanged when attention turns warm or cold.
translationLiteral: समः (equal) / शत्रौ (to enemy) / च (and) / मित्रे (to friend) / च (and) / तथा (likewise) / मानापमानयोः (in honor and dishonor) / शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु (in cold and heat, pleasure and pain) / समः (equal) / सङ्गविवर्जितः (free from attachment)
